text:
Olympic champion Thomas Ceccon was caught catching some Z's in a Parisian park following his public grievances about the conditions at the Olympic Village.

The Italian swimmer, who clinched gold in the men's 100m backstroke and bronze in the men's 4x100m freestyle at the Paris Games, did not hold back in criticising the "bad" living standards after missing out on a spot in the men's 200m backstroke final on Wednesday.

"There is no air conditioning in the village, it's hot, the food is bad," lamented Ceccon. "Many athletes move for this reason: it's not an alibi or excuse, it's the reality of what perhaps not everyone knows.

"I'm disappointed that I didn't make the final but I was too tired. It's hard to sleep both at night and in the afternoon. Usually, when I'm at home, I always sleep in the afternoon: here I really struggle between the heat and the noise."



Taking matters into his own hands, Ceccon was spotted by Saudi Arabian rower Husein Alireza taking a siesta outdoors, with Alireza captioning the footage: "Rest today, conquer tomorrow."

Ceccon joins a chorus of athletes airing their frustrations with the Olympic Village accommodations including Australian swimming sensation Ariane Titmus who, after her victory in the women's 400m freestyle, remarked: "It probably wasn't the time I thought I was capable of, but living in the Olympic Village makes it hard to perform.

"It's definitely not made for high performance, so it's about who can really keep it together in the mind."

Romanian table tennis star Bernadette Szocs has blasted the conditions at a competition, revealing to theGuardian: "There is no air conditioning, just this fan and it is not enough.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

"Somehow we were lucky that it was not so hot outside so we didn't need it so much but it is now hot and you can feel it is too hot in the room.

"[The fan] is not powerful enough and when it is pointing at you it is good but after it is turning you don't feel it. We are sleeping with the door open in the night. The rooms are small and we are two persons."

title: Olympic gold medallist sleeps in a park after Village complaints | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Matthew Cooper
description: Italian athlete Thomas Ceccon is not impressed by the living conditions at the Olympic Village.
